memory_candidates.content: The /sheet command (generic XLSX create/edit/analyze using openpyxl) exists in both the Zed repo and the nvim filetypes extension with identical content. The founder extension has a completely different /sheet command (cost breakdown with forcing questions). When porting commands between repos, always check if they already exist in a different extension before duplicating.

artifacts.summary: Research report: sheet command already exists in filetypes extension, three implementation options identified

active_projects.completion_summary: Fixed discord-link.lua session discovery to use correct opencode CLI field names (directory, id, title) instead of nonexistent fields (working_directory, cwd, status, session_id, name). Replaced broken status-based fallback with most-recent-session fallback. Updated discord-session-picker.lua to prioritize correct field names while keeping bot API names as fallbacks.

Revised implementation plan (v2) with 7 phases incorporating all 10 round 2 research findings
Implementation summary for Neovim Discord integration with 3 phases completed

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode has a comprehensive headless/CLI API: 'opencode serve' starts a persistent server with basic auth (OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD), 'opencode run --command' executes named commands programmatically, 'opencode attach' connects to remote servers, and 'opencode session list/delete' manages session lifecycle. The --format json flag enables machine-parseable output. This client-server architecture means OpenCode agents can be controlled from external services like Discord bots or webhooks without the Neovim TUI.

For Discord bot development on NixOS, nextcord (python3Packages.nextcord, v3.1.1) is the optimal choice: actively maintained (Aug 2025), slash-command-native with decorator-based API, async/aiohttp-based, and available directly in nixpkgs without flake inputs. Prefer nextcord over discord.py for new projects due to more frequent updates and better Python 3.12+ support.

active_projects.completion_summary: Implemented Neovim-side Discord integration with session linking command (:OpenCodeLinkDiscord / <leader>ar) and Telescope session picker (:DiscordSessions / <leader>aD). Both modules communicate with the Discord bot HTTP API using async curl requests.

active_projects.description: Investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills to enable CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing, following corrected understanding of fork mechanics from official Claude Code docs

artifacts.summary: Revised plan to investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills, enabling FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing with 4 phases: audit, pilot, rollout, documentation

Definitive architecture analysis: hybrid inline-agent approach has no precedent, context: fork + orchestration IS proven (slide skills), cache sharing is fundamentally incompatible with named agent routing, recommend rescoping task 500 to documentation-only

memory_candidates.content: All loaded extension skills (neovim-research, neovim-implementation, nix-research, nix-implementation) use the same Pattern A delegation as core skills -- explicit Task tool with subagent_type and multi-stage postflight. They do NOT use context: fork or agent: frontmatter. Only unloaded extension skills (e.g., slide-planning, slide-critic) use Pattern B with context: fork.

The present extension's skill-slide-planning and skill-slide-critic skills use context: fork + agent: in frontmatter AND explicit subagent_type in their Task call. This proves that context: fork with multi-stage orchestration (preflight, interactive Q&A, delegation, postflight) works correctly.
Claude Code agent definitions support a skills field that preloads skill content into the subagent context at startup. This is described as the inverse of context: fork.

active_projects.description: Optimize team-mode skills (team-research, team-plan, team-implement) to maximize FORK_SUBAGENT parallel cache sharing benefits for teammates 2-N

artifacts.summary: Team-mode skills are already eligible for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing; optimization focuses on prompt restructuring, dynamic token multiplier calculation, and cache-aware metadata reporting.
4-phase plan to restructure team-mode prompts for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing, replace hardcoded token multiplier with dynamic formula, and update documentation

memory_candidates.content: TeammateTool spawns in team-mode skills do NOT specify subagent_type, making them eligible for CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing. Teammates 2-N inherit the parent's prompt cache prefix, reducing input token cost by ~90%. Both CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 must be enabled. The actual cache benefit depends on the byte-identical shared prefix ratio -- prompts should put shared content first and teammate-specific assignments last to maximize overlap.

skill-team-research hardcodes team_size=4 in Stage 1 regardless of user-provided --team-size flag. The four fixed roles are: Primary (A), Alternatives (B), Critic (C), Horizons (D). The token_usage_multiplier is hardcoded at 5.0 in all team skill metadata but should be dynamically calculated.
